name: design surfaces: a public REST API gets a gateway, rate limiting, request validation, and versioning grounded in the Microsoft REST API guidelines
command:
cat > p.json <<'JEOF'
{"complete":true,"answers":{"workload":"x","criticality":"mission-critical","availability_tolerance":"minutes","data_loss_tolerance":"minutes","data_sensitivity":"regulated","compliance_regime":"hipaa","scale":"large","budget_posture":"balanced","consistency_need":"strong","communication_style":"event-driven","data_volume":"large","read_write_pattern":"analytics","integration_scope":"public","data_cadence":"real-time"}}
JEOF
bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/business-translate.sh" --profile p.json --out req.json --now 2026-06-08T12:00:00Z 2>/dev/null
node -e 'const j=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("req.json","utf8"));const has=(p,c)=>(j.pillars[p]||[]).some(x=>x.concern===c);const src=(p,c)=>((j.pillars[p]||[]).find(x=>x.concern===c)||{}).source_id;process.exit(has("api","rest_api_gateway")&&has("api","rate_limiting")&&has("api","request_validation")&&has("api","api_versioning")&&src("api","rest_api_gateway")==="microsoft-rest-api-guidelines"?0:1)' && echo rest 1>&2
